popularStory.headline: BIO
popularStory.description:
BIO:  from Wikipedia listing for Inés de Ayala as of 9/16/2016

Inés de Ayala y Toledo ( m. Toledo, 4 de septiembre 1453) IV señora de Casarrubios del Monte, Provincia de Toledo, fue hija de Pedro Suá …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ines Alfonso de Ayala於大約1328年出生在西班牙時，她的父親Fernán Pérez Ayala, IX señor de Ayala24歲，母親Elvira Álvarez de Ceballos19歲。她和Diego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayor至少有2個兒子和5個女兒。她去世於1403年之後在西班牙劍橋大學默里 卡斯利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ines Alfonso de Ayala于大约1328年出生在西班牙时，她的父亲Fernán Pérez Ayala, IX señor de Ayala24岁，母亲Elvira Álvarez de Ceballos19岁。她和Diego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayor至少有2个儿子和5个女儿。她去世于1403年之后在西班牙劍橋大學默里 卡斯利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ayala
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Manuel, Jesus, Miguel, Pedro, Francisco, Raul, Jorge, Rafael.
Basque: Castilianized form of Aiala, habitational or topographic name from ai ‘slope, hillside’ + al(h)a ‘pasture’.

nameMeanings.name: Ines
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Agnes  , now also used, usually without the accent, in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Agnes
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Alfonso
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Visigothic: probably a compound of adal ‘noble’ + funs ‘ready’; a traditional royal name.

en.lifeSummary: When Ines Alfonso de Ayala was born about 1328, in Spain, her father, Fernán Pérez Ayala, IX señor de Ayala, was 24 and her mother, Elvira Álvarez de Ceballos, was 19. She had at least 2 sons and 5 daughters with Diego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayor. She died after 1403, in New Castile, Spain.

fr.Malpica de Tajo, Toledo, Castilla-La Man: Malpica de Tajo, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ines Alfonso de Ayala est née environ 1328, à Espagne, son père, Fernán Pérez Ayala, IX señor de Ayala, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Elvira Álvarez de Ceballos, avait 19 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles avec Diego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayor. Elle est décédée après 1403, à New Castile, Espagne.

de.Malpica de Tajo, Toledo, Castilla-La Man: Malpica de Tajo, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien
de.lifeSummary: Als Ines Alfonso de Ayala um 1328 in Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Fernán Pérez Ayala, IX señor de Ayala 24 und ihre Mutter Elvira Álvarez de Ceballos 19. Sie hatte mit Diego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayor mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1403 in New Castile, Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ines Alfonso de Ayala è nata il circa 1328 nel Spagna, sua padre, Fernán Pérez Ayala, IX señor de Ayala, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Elvira Álvarez de Ceballos, aveva 19 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 5 figlie con Diego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayor. Morì dopo il 1403, nel New Castile, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Ines Alfonso de Ayalaが約1328年にスペインで生まれた時，父Fernán Pérez Ayala, IX señor de Ayalaは24歳，母Elvira Álvarez de Ceballosは19歳であった。彼女はDiego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayorとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1403年以後にスペインニュー カスティルで死去した。

ko.Malpica de Tajo, Toledo, Castilla-La Man: 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도 맬피카 데 타조
ko.lifeSummary: Ines Alfonso de Ayala(이)가 대략 1328년 스페인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Fernán Pérez Ayala, IX señor de Ayala(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elvira Álvarez de Ceballos(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 Diego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayor(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1403년 이후 스페인 뉴 카스틸에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ines Alfonso de Ayala nasceu aproximadamente 1328, em Espanha, seu pai, Fernán Pérez Ayala, IX señor de Ayala, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Elvira Álvarez de Ceballos, tinha 19 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas com Diego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayor. Ela faleceu após 1403, em New Castile, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ines Alfonso de Ayala родилась около 1328 г. в местности Испания, её отцу по имени Fernán Pérez Ayala, IX señor de Ayala было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Elvira Álvarez de Ceballos, 19. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей от Diego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayor. Она умерла после 1403 г. в местности ню кастиль, Испания.

Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 5 hijas con Diego Gómez de Toledo Señor de Casarrubios del monte, Notario y Alcalde Mayor. Murió después de 1403, en New Castile, España.
